text: Hi everyone, the video today will show the laparoscopic resection of a large rectosigmoid mass. Preoperative endoscopy failed in this lady twice because of the presence of a very tight rectosigmoid stricture. Although the mucosa looked normal, the stricture could not be negotiated. A CT scan and a CT colonoscopy showed the presence of thickened and inflamed recto-sigmoid mucosa with a very tight structure. Although a diagnosis of diverticular disease was made, malignancy could not be entirely ruled out.

The laparoscopic view reveals a large recto-sigmoid mass with phlegmon, adhered to the left tubovarian structures, peritoneum, and pelvic wall. Dissection begins at the sacral promontory, splitting the peritoneum. The inferior mesenteric artery is dissected from its origin at the aorta, with multiple lymph nodes visible and removed with the specimen. The apical lymph node is isolated and sent separately. The dissection proceeds laterally to medially, separating the mesocolon from retroperitoneal structures in the fascia of Toldt direction. The ureter is visible crossing over the left common iliac vessels, alongside clearly demonstrated gonadal vessels.

The surgeon develops the anterior plane between the rectum and vagina, using a narrow DVR retractor inserted in the vagina to aid dissection. The posterior TME plane is also developed, allowing the mass to be lifted in a bloodless plane. The peritoneum is pulled into the phlegmon or rectal mass and taken en bloc to ensure oncological principles are followed.

The surgeon removes the tubo-ovarian structures from the phlegmon, which are adhered by flimsy adhesions, without formal dissection. The left pelvic wall and peritoneum, adhered to the mass, are excised en bloc following oncological principles. Dissection is performed around the mid to lower rectum to prepare for the application of a linear cutter stapler.
The patient did very well post-operatively. The pathology showed this to be severe diverticular disease. No malignancy was found in the specimen. Thank you for watching.
